title: Greenhouse Cultivation & Global Species Master Pack

content.summary: Prized gourmet mushroom with thick, meaty stem and savory umami flavor. Ideal for greenhouse cultivation.
content.description: Large, thick cylindrical white stem with a small flat brown cap. Excellent shelf life and firm culinary texture resembling scallops.
content.identificationGuide: Cultivated on sterilized hardwood sawdust supplemented with wheat bran. Fruiting requires 15-20°C, 85-90% relative humidity, and high fresh air exchange.
content.habitatDesc: Cultivated indoors/greenhouses on hardwood sawdust blocks or straw bags.
content.medicinalDesc: Contains lovastatin for cholesterol regulation and high levels of ergothioneine antioxidant.

sourcesAttribution.notes: Greenhouse fruiting parameters: Temp 15-20°C, RH 85-90%, CO2 < 1200 ppm.

media.caption: Thick meaty stems of King Oyster

content.summary: Classic savory gourmet & medicinal mushroom grown on oak logs or hardwood bags in greenhouses.
content.description: Umbrella-shaped brown cap with white flocking/scales along the margin. Gills are white to pale cream, attached to a central tough fibrous stem.
content.identificationGuide: Cultivated on oak/hardwood logs or supplemented sawdust blocks. Requires cold shock (soaking in cold water 12-24h) to trigger heavy fruiting flushes at 16-22°C.
content.habitatDesc: Hardwood logs (Oak, Maple) or greenhouse sawdust blocks.
content.seasonalityDesc: Spring to autumn outdoors, year-round in greenhouses.
content.toxicityDesc: Edible and highly nutritious. Cook thoroughly.
content.medicinalDesc: Contains Lentinan, a beta-glucan proven to enhance immune cell function and lower blood pressure.

sourcesAttribution.notes: Cultivated for over 1,000 years in East Asia.

content.summary: Large, rosette-like cluster of greyish-brown caps with intense savory aroma and medicinal D-fraction.
content.description: Forms large dense clusters of overlapping feather-like caps attached to a branching central core. Pore underside is white.
content.identificationGuide: Greenhouse culture uses oak sawdust supplemented with rice bran. Requires 15-20°C and indirect light for cap pigmentation.
content.habitatDesc: At base of mature Oak trees or greenhouse sawdust bags.
content.seasonalityDesc: Autumn in wild, year-round in greenhouse.
content.medicinalDesc: Contains Maitake D-Fraction, widely researched for anti-tumor and blood glucose regulating effects.

media.caption: Dense rosette cluster of Maitake caps

content.summary: High-value bright orange club-shaped medicinal fungus cultivated on sterile grain substrates in climate-controlled rooms.
content.description: Slender, vibrant orange club-like fruiting bodies (2-7 cm tall) covered in tiny pimple-like perithecia openings.
content.identificationGuide: Cultivated indoors/greenhouses on rice/broth mixtures inside glass jars under LED lighting at 18-22°C.
content.medicinalDesc: Rich in Cordycepin (adenosine analogue) and adenosine, supporting cellular ATP production, stamina, and oxygen uptake.

sourcesAttribution.notes: Renowned energy and lung vitality tonic.

media.caption: Vibrant orange Cordyceps clubs in jar culture

content.summary: Robust burgundy-capped mushroom that thrives in greenhouse soil beds, woodchips, and outdoor garden mulch.
content.description: Large wine-red to reddish-brown cap fading to tan. Gills start pale, turning dark purplish-grey with age. Thick stem features a cogwheel-like ring.
content.identificationGuide: Easy garden/greenhouse bed species. Inoculate woodchips or straw beds alongside greenhouse crops. Fruits at 15-25°C.
content.habitatDesc: Woodchip beds, straw mulch, and greenhouse floor soil beds.
content.toxicityDesc: Choice edible when young before caps flatten out.
content.medicinalDesc: Accumulates soil heavy metals safely while building rich humus soil for garden crops.

sourcesAttribution.notes: Ideal companion mushroom for greenhouse vegetable beds.

media.caption: Burgundy cap of Wine Cap in woodchips

content.summary: Ubiquitous medicinal bracket fungus featuring colorful concentric bands and tiny white pores underneath.
content.description: Thin, velvety bracket displaying concentric zones of brown, tan, blue, orange, and white. Underneath surface is bright white to cream with tiny pores (3-5 per mm).
content.identificationGuide: Check for VELVETY surface, multi-colored concentric rings, WHITE porous underside (does not stain brown when scratched), and thin flexible tough texture.
content.habitatDesc: Dead hardwood logs, stumps, and greenhouse hardwood sawdust bags.
content.toxicityDesc: Non-toxic, but tough and leathery. Prepared as tea or extract.
content.medicinalDesc: Source of PSK (Polysaccharide Krestin) and PSP, FDA-studied immuno-modulators used in adjunctive oncology care.

sourcesAttribution.notes: Extensive clinical studies on PSK immunomodulation.

media.caption: Concentric colorful rings of Turkey Tail
